# UC-22.11.109 · PCI DSS v4.0 8.3.10 — Certificate Revocation Checking Integrity Evidence

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security

*We continuously evidence PCI DSS v4.0 8.3.10 — that ISE OCSP/CRL revocation infrastructure is operational. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.*

---

## Description

We continuously evidence PCI DSS v4.0 8.3.10 — that ISE OCSP/CRL revocation infrastructure is operational.

## Value

When OCSP/CRL silently fail, ISE may fall back to allow — meaning revoked certs continue to authenticate. PCI 8.3.10 needs continuous proof this isn't happening. Daily archived evidence is the audit-quality artefact.

## Implementation

Daily.

## Evidence

Saved search archived to `audit_evidence` with PCI 8.3.10 tagging.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Block OCSP URL via firewall. Evidence captured.

### Negative scenario

OCSP/CRL healthy. Empty result; control attested.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.70 operational.

## Step 1 — Data

Via Cisco ISE add-on.

## Step 2 — Implementation

```ini
[UC-22.11.109: PCI 8.3.10 — Cert Revocation Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.11.109,reg=PCIDSS,clause=8.3.10
```

## Step 3-5 — Validate, Operationalize, Audit

Quarterly evidence pack for QSA review.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (35102, 35201, 35103) earliest=-1d
| stats count by ocsp_responder_url, crl_repository_url, MessageCode
| eval pcidss_clause="8.3.10", control="Cert_Revocation"
| collect index=audit_evidence
```

## Visualization

PCI 8.3.10 panel: OCSP/CRL availability gauge; failure trend.

## Known False Positives

1. Brief OCSP responder maintenance windows.
